kaos_elements.name: Safety Goal
kaos_elements.description: Safety objective for Library: Librarians must be able to add new books, remove books, and manage member accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): Librarians must be able to add new books, remove books, and manage member accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): A library management system must allow library members to borrow and return books
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): The system must track which books are available, which are borrowed, and by whom
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): Members must be able to search for books by title, author, or ISBN
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): The system must enforce borrowing limits (e
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): , maximum number of books per member) and track overdue books
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): Librarians must be able to add new books, remove books, and manage member accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

steps.analysis_text: The proposed library management system aligns with the sustainability and trustworthiness goals outlined. However, both sets of requirements are pending validation, indicating they have not yet been confirmed as satisfactory. There are potential conflicts between sustainability requirements focused on tracking resource usage and trustworthiness requirements emphasizing security and integrity.
steps.analysis: The proposed library management system aligns with the sustainability and trustworthiness goals outlined. However, both sets of requirements are pending validation, indicating they have not yet been confirmed as satisfactory. There are potential conflicts between sustainability requirements focused on tracking resource usage and trustworthiness requirements emphasizing security and integrity.
steps.feedback: Further refinement is needed to clarify overlaps between sustainability and trust requirements. Specifically, the descriptions of certain requirements should explicitly state how security measures will not hinder the tracking of resources for sustainability purposes.

steps.feedback: Consider ensuring that the enforcement of borrowing limits does not compromise member accessibility to books. Review the security protocols associated with member account management to enhance trustworthiness while maintaining user experience.

steps.analysis: The requirements for both trustworthiness and responsibility are well-aligned with the functionalities of the library management system. However, there are some overlapping descriptions that need to be clarified to ensure distinct roles for each requirement.
steps.feedback: It is recommended to clearly separate trustworthiness requirements related to system security from those concerned with responsibility, such as tracking and accountability. This will facilitate better understanding and implementation of the requirements.

steps.analysis: The trustworthiness requirements focus on ensuring members can securely search for books and that borrowing limits are enforced, while the responsibility requirements emphasize regulatory accountability and ethical compliance in managing books and member accounts.

steps.analysis_text: The proposed library management system requirements reflect a strong focus on responsibility and safety measures. Each responsibility requirement aligns with an equivalent safety requirement, indicating an integrated approach to design and implementation. However, all reviewer elements are currently pending, which suggests that no safety requirements have been validated yet, potentially leading to conflicts in system design and operational execution.

Each responsibility requirement aligns with an equivalent safety requirement, indicating an integrated approach to design and implementation. steps.analysis_text: The proposed library management system requirements reflect a strong focus on responsibility and safety measures. Each responsibility requirement aligns with ensuring that accountability is maintained among library members and that ethical compliance is met through librarian capabilities. Safety requirements complement these, ensuring that the system functions reliably and prevents hazards during operation.
steps.analysis: The proposed library management system requirements reflect a strong focus on responsibility and safety measures. Each responsibility requirement aligns with ensuring that accountability is maintained among library members and that ethical compliance is met through librarian capabilities. Safety requirements complement these, ensuring that the system functions reliably and prevents hazards during operation.
steps.feedback: It is essential to ensure that all requirements are clearly defined and that there is no overlap in responsibilities and safety measures to avoid confusion during implementation.
